product_name: Magnum Opus Scholarly Retrieval And Warrant System
final_object_type: local scholarly retrieval and evidence-warrant software
runtime_target: local corpus service with index, retrieval trace, scoring, negative-space, abstention, and citation/export layer
operational_end_state: A researcher can ask a question against a local scholarly corpus, inspect retrieved passages, see what the corpus does not support, receive an evidence score, and export only claims that remain inside the source warrant.
minimum_operational_release: A runnable slice of Magnum Opus Scholarly Retrieval And Warrant System: one complete workflow from input to output, with fixture, state record, export, acceptance test, and source-bound claim boundary.
full_build_definition: A runnable local or web application with a defined user flow, input fixture, persisted or exportable output, source-bound mechanism, acceptance tests, and a reviewer-visible claim ceiling.
source_actions_before_claim: Carry 6 source-application role(s) into the first build release and convert the most load-bearing source into a close-read contract before any quotation, page-specific citation, or strong literature-positioning claim.
claim_boundary: This is a full-build plan, not a claim that the full operational system has already been completed.
public_claim_now: May claim a prototype-to-full-build path exists; do not claim completed operational system.
current_status: specified proof plan; needs live or replayable demonstrator
first_full_build: Build a public-safe retrieval trace with corpus boundary, retrieved passages, evidence score, negative-space finding, abstention, and claim ceiling.
launch_or_review_path: Create or open the local runtime slice for `native-magnum-opus`, then execute the fixture-to-output workflow and capture the result.

instruments.purpose: Turn the `corpus registry` module into a concrete input-operation-output step for Magnum Opus Scholarly Retrieval And Warrant System.
instruments.input_contract: Project fixture, source duty, current claim boundary, and required output state.
instruments.operation: Execute or inspect the corpus registry step against the project fixture and record the state change it is supposed to produce.
instruments.output_contract: A reviewer-visible state record, output artifact, or decision boundary tied to the project acceptance tests.
instruments.acceptance_condition: The corpus registry instrument is present, named, connected to the fixture, and has an output or decision field that can be reviewed.

acceptance_tests: claim ceiling test: public claims cannot exceed built evidence
source test: metadata-routed sources require close reading before citation or quotation
privacy test: no local paths or private directories in public deploy
retrieval replay test: clean and contaminated fixtures produce inspectable traces
state-transition test: visible input changes recorded state and output

fixture.fixture_name: Magnum Opus Scholarly Retrieval And Warrant System first operational slice
fixture.input: Build a public-safe retrieval trace with corpus boundary, retrieved passages, evidence score, negative-space finding, abstention, and claim ceiling.
fixture.operation: Run the room engine through each required instrument, then produce a reviewer-visible state record and claim boundary.
fixture.expected_output: A runnable slice of Magnum Opus Scholarly Retrieval And Warrant System: one complete workflow from input to output, with fixture, state record, export, acceptance test, and source-bound claim boundary.

export_schema.instrument_results: one result object per required module
export_schema.acceptance_results: one result object per required test
export_schema.claim_boundary: public claim ceiling after this run
export_schema.full_underlying_system_complete: always false unless backed by actual external runtime evidence
